title: The name of the tab
checkLabels.t3: title must have text in it
checkLabels.t4: The heading must still be there
checkLabels.t5: The sentence must still be there
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of naming it. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.no-title: The tab still says "index.html". Nobody bookmarks a tab like that. Give the page a name.
why.title-not-in-head: The title is there, but not in head. In body the browser moves it for you, but your code is lying — and some tools will miss it.
why.empty-title: The tag is there, but it is empty. The tab will stay nameless.
why.lost-content: Something is gone from the page. You are naming the tab, not deleting content. The heading and the sentence stay.
hint2.no-title: The tag is title. It goes inside <head>, exactly as you saw in the example.
hint2.title-not-in-head: Look at where you put it. Is it between <head> and </head>, or between <body> and </body>?
hint3.title-not-in-head: <title> is information ABOUT the page — what it is called. So its place is in the invisible half.
hint2.empty-title: There must be text between <title> and </title>. <title>My page</title>, not <title></title>.
hint2.lost-content: Compare with the original code. The heading and the sentence must be inside <body>.
blocks.0.text: Look at the browser tab above. What does it say?
blocks.1.text: "index.html". Or nothing. Or some path to a file. Nobody bookmarks a tab like that. Nobody finds it later among twenty others.
blocks.2.text: The empty head is needed now
blocks.3.text: Remember <head> — the part that is not visible? So far it sat empty and looked pointless. Here is the first thing that goes into it.
blocks.5.text: Run. The tab above changes. Nothing visible in the page has changed — but something did.
blocks.7.text: The tab. Bookmarks. History. The Google result — the blue line people click on. All of them read <title>.
blocks.8.text: None of them looks at your page. They read only this one word in the head.
blocks.9.text: "I do not write HTML for the browser. I write it for those who do not look at the screen."
